summary.intent: Fix inline review comments on PR #541 in the moltis repo, addressing issues in web_search.rs and firecrawl.rs.
summary.outcome: Fixed two issues: Firecrawl early-return preventing key injection via with_firecrawl_config(), and a timeout race condition. All 641 tests pass and changes were committed, pushed, and review threads resolved.

repo: Firecrawl tool construction should never early-return on missing API key since the key may arrive later via with_firecrawl_config() or runtime env provider — execute() handles empty keys gracefully.
To avoid HTTP client teardown racing the server response, the Firecrawl API body timeout should be slightly shorter than the HTTP client timeout.
truncate_at_char_boundary is pub(crate) in web_fetch.rs and shared with firecrawl.rs to avoid duplication.
DEFAULT_BASE_URL is pub(crate) in firecrawl.rs and used everywhere instead of hardcoded strings.
with_firecrawl emits tracing::warn! when fallback is enabled but no key is found, and sets firecrawl_fallback = false.

code.finding: Firecrawl branch in from_config_with_env_overrides was early-returning before with_firecrawl_config() could inject the API key; fixed by always constructing the tool.
API request body timeout set slightly shorter than HTTP client timeout to allow structured error responses before connection teardown.

workflow: After pushing fixes, GitHub review threads must be explicitly resolved via API — they don't auto-resolve on push.
Ran cargo fmt + clippy before tests as a pre-commit hygiene step.
PR comments exist at two levels: inline review comments and issue-level (top-level) comments — both need to be checked separately.

friction: Had to distinguish between inline review comments and issue-level PR comments via separate API calls to find all open feedback.
The Firecrawl early-return bug was subtle — the key injection happens post-construction, so the missing-key guard was too eager.
